sections.paragraph:
adv., there (Arab. ثَمَّ and of time ثُمَّ; Chald. תָּם; Syriac ܬܳܡܰܢ. A trace of another form שֹׁם = ثُمَّ is found in the pr.n. גֵּרְשֹׁם Exo 2:22, as to which see p. 182, A . In the Indo-Germanic languages there answer to this, Gr. τῆμος, tunc; Lat. tum (tunc; compare num, nunc); Anglosax. thoeœnne; whence the English then; Germ. dann; all of which are applied to time; see No. 2). It is used-

(1) pr. of place,-(a) i.q. eo loco, ἐκεῖ. Gen 2:8, 12 11:2 , 31 Gen 11:31 12:7 , Gen 12:8, 10 Gen 12:10 13:4 , 18 Gen 13:18and so very often. When preceded by a relative, אֲשֶׁר שָׁם where, Exo 20:18 commonly with one or more words between, שָׁם-אֲשֶׁר Gen 13:3 2Sa 15:21. שָׁם-שָׁם here, there, Isa 28:10.-(b) after verbs of motion, i.q. שָׁמָּה thither (like ἐκεῖ for ἐκεῖσε), 1Sa 2:14 2Ki 19:32 whence שָׁם-אֲשֶׁר whither, 1Ki 18:10 Jer 19:14.

(2) used of time, at that time, then (like the Gr. ἐκεῖ, Lat. ibi, illico, compare the above remarks on the traces of this word in other languages), Psa 14:5 132:17 Jud. 5:11 .

(3) i.q. therein, in that thing, Hos 6:7, “they have transgressed the covenant, שָׁם בָּֽגְדוּ בִי therein (darin, in diefem Stücke) they have rebelled against me.”

With ה parag. שָׁ֫מָּה (read shammah)-(a) thither, Gen 19:20 23:13 Isa 34:15 (where we must render, “thither shall she place her nest,” etc., comp. Psa 122:5 Exo 29:42).-(b) i.q. שָׁם there (so that -ָה has a mere demonstrative power), Jer 18:2. After a relative, אֲשֶׁר שָׁ֫מָּה whither, Gen 20:13 rarely where, 2Ki 23:8.

With pref. מִשָּׁם thence-

(1) of place, Gen 2:10 11:8 , Gen 11:9 1Sa 4:4. מִשָּׁם-אֲשֶׁר whence, Deu 9:28.

(2) used of time, Hos 2:17.

(3) i.q. from that thing, whence, like the Lat. inde (unde), Gen 3:23, “that he might till the ground אֲשֶׁר לֻקַּה מִשָּׁם whence (out of which) he had been taken;” 1Ki 17:13, עֲשִׂי לִי מִשָּׁם עֻגָּה “make me thence (out of that meal) a cake;” Ezr 5:3. Pleonastically, Gen 49:24. מִשָּׁם רֹעֶה אֶבֶן יִשְׂרָאֵל “from thence (dorther), from the shepherd, the stone of Israel (comes),” etc.
